title: Rebutia nigricans
taxonomy: Rebutia nigricans is classified as a species of flowering plant in the cacti family, Cactaceae. It belongs to the genus Rebutia and is native to Bolivia. Within the Rebutia genus, it is classified as a species of the subgenus Rebutia. The plant is further classified into the family Cactaceae, order Caryophyllales, and class Magnoliopsida. The full binomial classification is Rebutia nigricans, with the genus being the most specific classification.

intro: Rebutia nigricans, also known as the black-spined Rebutia, is a species of cactus in the genus Rebutia, characterized by its dark, almost black, spines and vibrant yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in treating various ailments such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Rebutia nigricans include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Rebutia nigricans is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it is also used in some traditional remedies as a dried powder or tincture.
